questions.question: Find the discriminant of the quadratic equation $2x^2 - 5x + 3 = 0$ and state the nature of its roots.
questions.answer: The discriminant is $1$, and since $D > 0$, the equation has two distinct real roots.

steps: Identify the coefficients $a$, $b$, and $c$ from the standard form $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$.
Calculate the discriminant $D = b^2 - 4ac$ and interpret its value to determine the nature of the roots.
solution.derivation: $a = 2, b = -5, c = 3 \\ D = b^2 - 4ac = (-5)^2 - 4(2)(3) \\ D = 25 - 24 = 1 \\ \text{Since } D > 0, \text{ the equation has two distinct real roots.}$

retrieved_sources: Chapter_3 (Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables).pdf
